title: Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering
summary: Retrieval Review: Accessible HTML Engineering retrieves prior skills in an original changed civic-service accessibility release scenario and requires implementation, behavior, failure-state, and transfer evidence.
objectives: Apply accessibility disability models, accessibility tree, keyboard focus, accessible names descriptions, aria use, accessible errors status, accessible media equivalents, accessibility test strategy in unfamiliar constraints.
Produce or audit a real changed civic-service accessibility release artifact without happy-path-only reasoning.
Preserve earlier requirements while testing keyboard, narrow-width, invalid, and assistive-semantic states.

starterFiles.html: <!-- Continue and harden the Community Needs Survey artifact here. -->

options.text: changed civic-service accessibility release phase 1.1: name the real task and surrounding conditions
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 1.2: identify where perception, input, comprehension, or motion can fail
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 1.3: connect barriers to permanent, temporary, situational, and overlapping needs
changed civic-service accessibility release phase 1.4: change the product constraint and verify the task with affected users and tools

options.text: Changed civic-service accessibility release case 2: screen readers announce exactly the visible text and ignore programmatic semantics.

Changed civic-service accessibility release case 2: add aria-label to every node so DOM semantics no longer matter.
